published_prior_art.cite: EdmondsMatchingPolytope1965 and Wolsey1975: finite polyhedral certificate machinery for matching/0-1 systems

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Repo axis is only incremental versus pid_network_exposure/v1's capacitated-incidence certificate conjecture; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship unless the draft isolates a strict joint direct/indirect certificate frontier not implied by the prior scalar ADE active-demand certificate template.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The general K-label certificate is a direct generalization of the banked shared-completion certificate motif plus standard finite 0-1 certificate machinery, so it reads as an extension rather than a flagship-new kernel.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The second branch assumes t<=h_cell,K(u;P) implies a witness in Omega_t(u,P), but support domination alone does not imply exact attainability; add t in E_cell(u,P) or W_t nonempty.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The iff certificate statement needs t to be cellwise attainable, not only h_2(u;P)<t<=h_cell,2(u;P); otherwise the active-basis family may be empty and the certificate claim becomes vacuous.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The t<=h_cell,K branch again needs explicit cellwise endpoint attainability before sparse-network incompatibility certificates in B_K(u,t,P) are defined.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Patch Theorem 1 and Conjectures 1/3 by replacing t<=h_cell with t in E_cell(u,P), or by proving the directional endpoint image is an interval under the stated lifted relaxation.

To meet the flagship floor, state a theorem-level frontier that is not inherited from pid_network_exposure/v1: e.g. a strict nonrectangular direct/indirect support certificate that cannot be reduced to scalar ADE endpoint demands.
Add a K>2 noncorner witness or downgrade Conjecture 3 to a future extension; the current general-K statement is too close to generic finite MIP certificate existence.

Anchor the flagship gap to a precise Schroder et al. result or open limitation beyond Sections 2--3, ideally a theorem/equation and a strict containment statement for the same threshold exposure setting.

recommended_next_step: Revise the endpoint-attainability hypotheses and sharpen the novelty kernel beyond the banked pid_network_exposure certificate template before resubmission.
